anti_claims: This skill JSON seed does not flip source authority away from the current skill markdown projection.
Resolved operated-standard and acted-on-kind edges are structural route mappings, not proof the skill is complete or used correctly by agents.
Absent mechanism or concept neighbours are residual pressure, not evidence that no such neighbours exist.
Generated graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omitted: mechanism or concept neighbours not named by typed source mapping
omission_receipt.reason: Skill instance preserves public-safe markdown source identity, digest, and typed route mapping only. JSON parity does not prove full skill-body completeness or runtime correctness.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Bind skill.uses mechanism and skill.applies concept edges only when source evidence names ids.

justification.summary: This markdown binds concept authoring to std_microcosm_concept, the concept doctrine kind, and the executable doctrine grammar mechanism because concepts must enter as typed mistake-prevention boundaries with source refs, specimen routes, payload shape, omission receipts, and anti-claims rather than as glossary prose.

This markdown binds concept authoring to std_microcosm_concept, the concept doctrine kind, and the executable doctrine grammar mechanism because concepts must enter as typed mistake-prevention boundaries with source refs, specimen routes, payload shape, omission receipts, and anti-claims rather than as glossary prose.
Skill source mapping names this concept as an applied doctrine boundary.
Skill source mapping names this mechanism as an operational dependency.

skill_payload.mapping_basis: This markdown binds concept authoring to std_microcosm_concept, the concept doctrine kind, and the executable doctrine grammar mechanism because concepts must enter as typed mistake-prevention boundaries with source refs, specimen routes, payload shape, omission receipts, and anti-claims rather than as glossary prose.

title: Microcosm Concept Author
triggers: A concept candidate needs governed admission rather than remaining a route label, glossary note, or untyped pressure handle.
The agent can name source text, population specimen, payload shape, relationship shape, paired mechanism boundary, omission receipt, and residual pressure for any unproven selective edge.
workflow: Read std_microcosm_concept, atlas/entry_packet concept-mechanism specimens, existing concept JSON rows, relation registry, and generated health before editing.
Admit only source-backed concept rows with specimen and payload-shape evidence; keep missing principle, mechanism, or axiom neighbours as typed residual pressure.
Regenerate the concept corpus and doctrine lattice, then run concept, standard, skill, paper-module, and broad projection checks before scoped commit.
